content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES OF

RONALD E. ISLES

RONALD E. ISLES
JONATHAN BARRY SEXTON

ATTORNEYS AT LAW
595 WEST LAMBERT ROAD
SUITE 208

OF COUNSEL

BREA, CALIFORNIA 92621
(714) 256-8835
FAX: (714) 529-4076

PAUL R. ROPER
EDWARD J. HAGGERTY
RICHARD K. ISLES

PARALEGALS

ANA M. MILLER
CONNIE S. HYLTON

& ASSOCIATES

AFR | 9 || 08 A* '92

March 27, 1992

California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Reference:

Annual Statement of Economic Interests 1991/92
Ronald E. Isles, Brea City Councilman

Commission Members:

Attached hereto is my 1991-92 Form 721, Statement of Economic Interests. In the preparation
of my report for this annual reporting period, several questions have arisen which require some
clarification. These questions relate to Schedule H-2 Income and Loans to a Business Entity or
Trust and Schedule H-3 Income from Rental Property to a Business Entity or Trust.

The first question relates to Schedule H-2 Income and Loans to a Business Entity or Trust for
business entity Town and Country Partners, a California general partnership in which I have a
50% interest (with the other 50% held by Don McBride). The assets held by this partnership
include three separate pieces of real property, all of which are disclosed on my Schedule C-1
(Interest in Real Property Held By a Business Entity or Trust). In late 1988, the interests in
these three properties were apportioned between the partners in the partnership, with the
property at 800 South Brea Boulevard in Brea apportioned to Don McBride, and the remaining
two properties apportioned to me. Responsibility for the debts related to each property was
apportioned to the partners in a like manner on the same date. For this reason, the outstanding
loans on the property at 800 South Brea Boulevard in Brea (apportioned to Don McBride) have
not appeared on my previous 721 Forms for the years 1989 and 1990. In the interest of caution,
we have included the outstanding loans on this property (from Igor Olenikoff and First Interstate
Bank) on my 1991-92 Form 721. Is it required that these loans be reported, and if so, should
appropriate amendments be made to my 1989 and 1990 Statements of Economic Interest?

The second question relates to Schedule H-3 Income From Rental Property for the business
entity Ronald E. Isles & Associates. Ronald E. Isles and Associates served as the master tenant
on the lease of the building located at 595 West Lambert Road in Brea in 1989, 1990 and 1991.
During that time, Ronald E. Isles & Associates collected the rents from various unit tenants
in the building and made a consolidated rental payment to the lessor, including its portion of the
monthly lease obligation. Since these receipts from other building tenants were merely pass-


2

through payments turned immediately over to the lessor of the building, they have not been
included on my past Statements of Economic Interest. Again, in the interest of caution, we have
reported these pass-through rent payments on my 1991-92 Statement of Economic Interests. Are
payments of this type required to be reported, and if so, should appropriate amendments be
made to my 1989 and 1990 Statements of Economic Interest?

Please forward your response to these questions to me at the offices of Ronald E. Isles &
Associates, 595 West Lambert Road, Brea, California. If your clarification of these issues
requires that I make amendments to my previous Statements of Economic Interest, please
forward the appropriate amendment forms to me at the same address. I thank you for your time
and consideration, and look forward to hearing from you.

Sincerely,

Ronald E. Isles & Associates

[signature]

Ron Isles
Brea City Councilman

Enclosure

I'm unable to transcribe the content of this image as it appears to be almost entirely blank or too faint to read clearly. The image shows what looks like a mostly white/blank page with only some very faint marks or artifacts visible in the upper right corner that are too unclear to transcribe accurately.

sections.question: First question: Business entity Town and Country Partners - In late 1988, interests in three properties were apportioned between partners, with one property apportioned to Don McBride and two to the requestor. Responsibility for debts related to each property was apportioned accordingly. The outstanding loans on the property apportioned to Don McBride (from Igor Olenikoff and First Interstate Bank) did not appear on previous Form 721s for 1989 and 1990, but have been included on the 1991-92 Form 721 in the interest of caution. Is it required that these loans be reported, and if so, should appropriate amendments be made to the 1989 and 1990 Statements of Economic Interest? Second question: Ronald E. Isles & Associates served as master tenant on a lease at 595 West Lambert Road in Brea in 1989, 1990, and 1991. During that time, the entity collected rents from various tenants and made consolidated rental payments to the lessor. These pass-through payments were not included on past Statements of Economic Interest but have been reported on the 1991-92 Statement in the interest of caution. Are payments of this type required to be reported, and if so, should appropriate amendments be made to the 1989 and 1990 Statements of Economic Interest?
sections.question_synthetic: A Brea City Councilman seeks clarification on two Schedule H reporting requirements: whether loans on a partnership property apportioned to another partner must be reported on his Form 721, and whether pass-through rental payments collected and immediately remitted by his business entity must be reported as rental income.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/inquiry letter, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or answer section is present. The document contains two distinct questions about Schedule H reporting requirements.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from Ronald E. Isles, a Brea City Councilman, to the FPPC seeking clarification on two economic interest reporting issues related to his 1991-92 Form 721 filing.
